sourceMovie.title: American Reunion
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: American Reunion (2012) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jon Hurwitz's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If American Reunion clicked for you, 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after American Reunion?
faq.answer: Try 10 Things I Hate About You, Ted 2, Pitch Perfect 2—each shares DNA with American Reunion while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is American Reunion?
faq.answer: American Reunion (2012) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is American Reunion part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "American Pie Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
